[h2]What a mess of a disappointment[/h2] I hated the visuals of the game when they first showed it off. Just bright colors everywhere on everything and it makes no sense. I touched the server slam and felt it was not for me. People still told me to play the full game. There are some cool bits but overall, it's horrible design choice after horrible design choice. [b]TL;DR the game is NOT MY JAM and I do NOT LIKE.[/b]  Upon trying to PLAY THE GAME for the first time, you're strapped in to some LSD trip of a cutscene that sticks a bunch of unrelated stock images of things in front of you. As if it is some great worthwhile mystery. And then begins the yapping about all sorts of things that make no sense and aren't all that immersive nor interesting. In short, it explains how you can "die" and "return" but in a overly wordy way. What takes them 10 minutes to explain they could've done in 5. And they've already done it in their teaser trailer stuff.  When you're finally out of it, you're stuck listening to more yapping while unable to move. It's got to do with the "story" so people will look past it. You then explore this nonsense area full of bright neon colors that no grand corporation would waste time coloring everything like that. You kill or get killed and your tutorial is over. After which you're bombarded with neigh endless handholding for each menu and what everything does.  Want to go to your Vault and see what's there? Learn how to use things?  TOO BAD, it will lead you by the nose to each and every little thing. Like the Details button and how it shows you details of things. WOW, I so needed to stop having any chance to learn as I go myself to drop everything and learn that highly valuable piece of information. It's as if they've never made any onboarding process before.  It's like how in Destiny 2, they continued to shove every notice into your face without giving you a moment to breathe.  [h2]What a SHELL of a game[/h2] I hate the Shells. It does not make sense to me that in a world where you can print an entire temporary body that most of them are the goofiest looking things imaginable. It also does not make sense to me that, You can select a Shell that you like but since it comes with pre-baked abilities, that's how it plays. Yes, I know, it's a Class system found in many other games but it's pants on head here. I dislike it entirely as it limits player freedom and choice for a system that is so undercooked that it's raw. Why are there only 7 (8 if you include ROOK) Shells available? They added a new one for Season 2 but I just do not see the appeal. You have humans using synthetic bodies but why not have robotic hybrids? Or entirely Robotic Shells?  Why can't I just select an appearance and then select an architype instead? I don't want to play as Babies first action figure with harmless rounded edges everywhere. Or some derpy mannequin with a hood.  Maybe I want to play as that X mouthed Statue of liberty girl? But I just don't like her kit.  [h2]Customizing is Horrendous[/h2] Everything that you can change or customize has been stripped away for a more curated experience. This means that you either do without, slowly unlock or PAY your way through.  Shells can only be modified by selecting a "Style" of sorts. Most of the styles come with only a color change and some have a slight visual change. Instead of doing things in the vain of Overwatch where you have a TON appearances for your Selected Shell and it comes with either color options or you can freely change the colors, you're stuck with whatever horrible color combinations a hap-hazard dev choose. I hate this to no end. Even if I select a Shell that I think looks stupid visually, I can't even make them my own. I can't change these terrible colors. I have to BUY their preselected nonsense. Here's a skin that makes you almost entirely RED with "Big Knee and Little Knee". The only cool feature you have for this Shell style is the head.  Did they learn nothing from Destiny or Halo? Did they forget in Halo 3 you could choose whatever color you pleased and change all sorts of armor appearances? That the ones you unlock were cooler than the default ones? Or in Destiny we could buy and apply cooler color combos to things?  Take a hard look at the Shell style options, they're completely pathetic and almost devoid of purpose. I don't want your lame color selections, I want to make this my own.  [h3]Weapons? Forget about it[/h3] Go look at your appearance choices for a Melee knife. They're all terrible. And all you have as a choice is a single type of Combat Knife.  Most guns look like they're all modular nonsense but I often do not see them used in that way. A piece seems to never be used elsewhere on any other weapon. So most of them look like they're trying to be Megablock but guns. You only have a few that pass the vibe check like the Combat Shotgun but even then, they still look very blocky. Like they were made for a voxel game and not a 250 Million dollar extraction shooter.  [h3]Customizing your Profile is just as bad[/h3] It actually feels like Bungie has gone out of their way to make everything as ugly as possible. Inventory UI? Everything is blocks and takes their sweet time revealing lootable items. On screen UI? Just looks like they stole the Destiny U. You have Profile Badges but you have to unlock them or buy them. Most of them are ♥♥♥♥. You have Profile Backgrounds but again, you unlock ♥♥♥♥ ones or buy ♥♥♥♥ ones. All of them are ♥♥♥♥. You have Weapon charms but again, it's all ♥♥♥♥. Borderlands 3 tried this and it failed because most people didn't care and the ones available were mediocre at best.   [h1]What about their fancy new PvE mode?[/h1] Oh you mean Player vs Experimental? Ha. Guess what? It's just as ♥♥♥♥ as the rest of the game. Other than any spooky creatures attacking me out of no where, it's just the same Robots you can find elsewhere. All on a map where you can't see for ♥♥♥♥ and cannot explore because anomalies.

How about AI combatants that can make you out clearly through fog and 80%+ cover and and then suddenly have 3-5 buddies, some not visible and some carrying energy shields, just zerg you? ("engaging and meaningful enemy contact")  Does that all sound good? Sweet! Now, add rat rejects from ARC Raiders and other extraction shooters who couldn't hack it elsewhere trying to prove how amazing of a gamer they are.  You have Marathon.  Marathon is a game where a colony ship is lost for a century. Suddenly, it starts broadcasting a message, so the Sol-system-based Humans on Earth and Mars decide to start sending your mind to "biomechanical" body "Shells" of questionable design so you, as the disembodied controller of this body, can act as their remote eyes, ears, and bringers of chaos for the various corporations and one "anti-corporation," called "Factions," who want you to pillage and scavenge for them. For this, they will pat you on the back and give you more guns that you're going to lose on your next jump-in anyways.  I am not saying that Marathon is a BAD game. The controls tend to be very solid and on-point. There are 9 "shells" that determine your class and abilities, though I haven't really explored this at all and none look interesting IMHO, and one class that's basically a dedicated, "I'm going in to kill players," shell. It kinda works and I get it. The gunplay is... Well, it's freaking Bungie. They know how to make in-game guns feel like actual weapons. There is a story and it seems like it might be interesting -- but it's buried under a BRUTAL shooter that takes many of the issues of old AI enemies and slaps a fresh coat of paint on it and all but DEMANDS players be PvP at the same time.  But now we look at the REST of the game. The AI combatants? I was liking the idea originally -- and then I had to deal with them. I have had multiple instances where the AI combatants were engaging with another player and I was simply observing the situation, only to start being shot by the same AI once their original target ducked behind a corner that would have had no reason to realize that I was present since I hadn't engaged and I was behind walls with the intent of avoiding combat. Ditto with the AI seeming to know where you are once you've triggered their combat system but remain within their leashing range.  The Exfil system: Single-use exit points that may or may not spawn in again once the original has been used, or you can go to the "contested" exit and be shot to death anyways by the combat groups that drop in. And if you DO manage to make it to the non-contested exits, you're going to be shot by players who are more interested in killing you than anything else. The UI: It ONLY makes sense if you pretend you're using a controller. I'm on PC. The art style: Natural areas look AMAZING! But why does a Robot race have everything labeled with with words that identify what the item is? I rolled my eyes when I saw something repeatedly labeled "SEAL." It was literally the seal between 2 sections of building. The Exfil itself feels entirely unnecessary. There is no real urgency to make it out alive because everything you did is already recorded. Sure, you don't get to keep your gear, but most likely you went in with a "sponsored" kit anyways so there is no attachment to a particular play or weapon format outside of what the game shoves in your hand to start with and the what the Hero -- erm, I mean Shell does. Doing a mission? Do the mission parts, then walk up to a robot and be shot to death. Save yourself the time because you're probably not gonna find weapons while you're out there outside of a charging pistol and the cash value of the junk doesn't justify the headache. But the WORST part of all? Even when I succeeded in exfiltrating or winning a PvP bout, it just was not fun. I have 365 hours on ARC Raiders because I had FUN. I have thousands of hours across the Halo and Destiny universes because they were FUN! Helldivers 2 was FUN but I have so much to do. Nothing in Marathon seemed FUN. I had nothing to care about in this game and that's a freaking shame.   So, unless you're planning on jumping in with a pair of friends and REALLY like having to fight your way to exits, I'm gonna have to call this a pass for now. It's a shame, too, because Bungie has a SOLID foundation of a game here and there is clearly a lot to explore. I would like to explore more of that, too -- but I know it's not going to be fun. Maybe they'll put out a book and save me the headache of buying a game I just don't care to play in its current state -- even at 30% off.

Not Recommended  I wanted Marathon to succeed, but after spending time with it and reading community feedback, I honestly don't see a bright future for this game.  Unfortunately, almost everything around the shooting feels frustrating. The UI is cluttered, information is hard to read, and the game does a poor job of explaining its systems to new players. Instead of feeling rewarding, many matches feel like you're wasting time just to lose everything to players who already know every mechanic and map.  The extraction-shooter formula can be exciting, but Marathon doesn't do enough to make losses feel meaningful. Dying often feels less like a lesson and more like a punishment. Casual players are likely to bounce off quickly, while veterans will dominate the experience.  Technical issues, questionable design choices, and Bungie's damaged reputation aren't helping either. The community has been raising concerns for months, yet many of the same problems keep coming up in discussions.  Marathon has solid shooting mechanics, but good gunplay alone isn't enough to carry an entire game. Right now it feels like style over substance, with a confusing gameplay loop that struggles to justify the time investment.  Pros:  Good weapon feel Strong audio and visual presentation  Cons:  Confusing UI Poor onboarding Punishing progression Unfriendly to casual players Technical issues Lacks long-term appeal  I wanted to enjoy it, but after a few hours I found myself asking one question: Why would I play this instead of the other extraction shooters already on the market? Unfortunately, I never found a good answer. 👎

Sometimes, the game loading times that are so slow they make it seem as though it has frozen. The game has a number of bugs, including issues with body blocking from teammates, as well as some problems with stairs and fences around the map. The gameplay feels quite slow. I cannot run for very long, and I can only perform up to three slides before my stamina is used up, which then takes a while to recover. At times, running and sliding do not respond properly even when I am holding the buttons. The weapon animations also feel quite slow, which can make a difference during fights. Weapon switching is not always consistent either; for example, scrolling up three times on the mouse wheel does not always bring out the knife as expected. Some of the visual effects and animations are quite intense, and it might be helpful to include a warning for players who are sensitive to this. The colour choices, particularly the combination of green text and strong blue tones, can also be a bit hard on the eyes. Also, sometimes, the game loading times that are so slow they make it seem as though it has frozen. On a positive note, I did enjoy using the knife against bots and the Recon robot. Overall, the game did not fully appeal to me, and I would not personally choose to buy it in its current state. It feels like it could benefit from further refinement to match the standard of a paid game.

I think Destiny and the rest of Bungie's games, while no doubt being catered more towards the hardcore crowd, always had something for casuals and people who have lives outside of grinding. I can't see anything like that here, which is a huge loss for people like me who were looking forward to seeing a loot-based extraction shooter finally break the hardcore, sweaty, nolifers only PvP mold that they all seem to inhabit. Bungie has some of the best PvE FPS design in the industry and they were uniquely positioned to make a PvE extraction shooter that's actually interesting and engaging. They don't seem to have any interest in that, unfortunately, as the current "PvE" mode is very limited, and it's still PvP lite so there's a decent chance you get shot in the back by a rat anyway.  I wish there was a "neutral" review option because it's by no means a bad game. This game is a masterpiece of visual and sound design as well as gunplay. The atmosphere and general vibe of everything, as well as the lore, are all really appealing and it's a shame it's locked behind one of the sweatiest games I've ever played. The game is pretty fun, I have to admit. Nice optimization good AI rly good in that terms. However, the controls are really bad. It feels like the game was designed with controller support in mind, but at the same time it doesn't actually work well on a controller. It's like an awkward mix between controller and keyboard-and-mouse controls, and the overall implementation feels poorly thought out.  Looting is especially frustrating. You can't easily transfer items back into a crate, and removing attachments from weapons is unnecessarily cumbersome. On a controller, you can't hold RT and press A to multi-select items, which makes inventory management even more annoying.  There are also several strange design choices. You can't ping yourself when you're dead. If you're using the "hold to run" option, sprinting stops whenever you change direction, forcing you to press the run button again. Why does pressing X drop an item on the ground during a match, but place it in the vault when you're in the vault menu? The inconsistency is confusing.  Another issue is the character boost system. The section where you boost characters actually applies to all characters, but the button is positioned under the abilities section, making it look like it's only connected to the currently selected character. Because of that, I didn't want to commit resources and ended up waiting for a long time. If there was an explanation somewhere, it was poorly presented.  I also use an Xbox Elite Series 2 controller, and I can assign keyboard keys to controller this is great cuz i have 4 extra buttons on back. But game doesn't let me to do that for example you can't move with the analog and crouch with Ctrl at the same time. If you try to do it controls will freak out and it wont work you cant mix it fast so its unusable. This is first game this is the case. I don't understand why is working like that.  Finally, the game feels heavily monetized for something that isn't free-to-play. It has a battle pass, premium skins, and other microtransactions that require real money. If the game is going to follow a free-to-play monetization model, why isn't it free-to-play as well?   Some of these systems may have explanations behind them, but they are poorly communicated to players.  There are several other issues too, but these are the main ones that stood out to me. I rly hope devs will fix and change issues i presented here   -IT'S RLY ONE DAY OF WORK. -  Good and fun game but needs fixing.

Personally, I feel like too much time is spent fighting PvE opponents that do considerable damage and have a seemingly infinite number of reinforcements, making it difficult to really get into and enjoy the PvP side of the game.  I feel this comes from a lack of understanding of what makes PvE fun in extraction shooters. Personally, I feel like extraction shooters in the past have shown they can be used for two main things: creating sound traps and opportunities to reveal yourself to other players, and providing short challenges with a chance of rewarding extra resources. I feel like Marathon fails to do both.  At no time did I get discovered, or discover other players, based on their combat. The PvE encounters drained resources without allowing for outsmarting the AI or providing interesting combat opportunities like other extraction shooters do. To give a short example, in Arc Raiders the PvE does significant damage, but each enemy has a weakness that players can learn to exploit, giving them the upper hand in combat encounters and creating a fun experience. I did not get that same feeling of risk and reward from Marathon.  The few times my inventory wasn't instantly filled, which allowed me to stay longer on the map and engage in PvP, the encounters were very short. A quick knife combo or a sniper shot from a distance sealed the deal long before either opponent could get clever with their movements or abilities.  This, in combination with having to read what each item does, as they are visually hard to distinguish and often look the same aside from their assigned value, leads to the gameplay slowing down significantly.  All in all, the game failed to grab me like other extraction shooters such as Arc Raiders or Hunt: Showdown. Both examples of great extraction shooters I would quickly recommend over Marathon if someone were to ask for my recommendation.

Punishing arcade shooters usually don't perform well, add on top of that the niche genre of extraction shooters. I think if they wanted something serious and heavy toned they should've gone a bit more grounded, the aesthetic here seems to be whatever they could find on Pinterest under the futuristic tag. All of the character skins are just abysmal, no cohesion or vibe at all it's just mish mashes of missing textures, the guns and maps are fine.  Not to mention the menus were strange and obnoxious the first time around, but even after I figured out what everything did it's pretty obvious this is gonna need something else soon or it's gonna end up dead. The skill tree offering seasonal bonuses is extremely lame, those aren't rewards that I care about or would ever want. Make em craftable with materials I can find in game or something instead of just buying storage space for the season, only this current season.   The game doesn't seem to offer very engaging combat either, pretty much everyone I came across was hiding/ducking or straight up avoiding the fight. Balancing seems to be a massive issue and I would wager that's why people are so passive, but I had a couple of scenarios where I was outranged by a pistol and essentially 3 shot. That's fine but against an LMG there should be some worry of getting tagged or even feeling suppressed but rather I get stared down cause they just know it'll beat it. It's the stuff you wouldn't expect that makes you lose gunfights and in future cases it's just a "Oh, yeah I forgot that thing shoots 20 yards with a red dot but fits in one hand".  PvP games always have a lot of emotion with them but after closing this I just wished I played Crimson Desert instead
